title: Archiving Cyclic Projects without Losing the board
excerpt: I use projects in Monday Work Management to manage development of products. The products undergo a 2 year revision cycle and then rest for 2-3 years before we start them again. In order to update the new milestones but k…

posts.author: Cellblock-D (Marty Bartram)
posts.cookedHtml: I use projects in Monday Work Management to manage development of products. The products undergo a 2 year revision cycle and then rest for 2-3 years before we start them again. In order to update the new milestones but keep the old data my thought is that I must duplicate each board, archive the original board, and then update the milestones in the copy. Is there a better way to do this? I am trying not to have to recreate the board or change names on the projects. Thanks.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Cellblock-D (Marty Bartram)
posts.cookedHtml: Work management, Pro. Thanks. I need to remember to add that as there are big differences between pro and enterprise.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely! It helps to know to answer cleanly. About how many of these boards are you ‘reactivating’ at a time?

posts.author: Cellblock-D (Marty Bartram)
posts.cookedHtml: One per month, occasionally two per month. For context each board represents a “text book”. The portfolio is approximately 15 books. Each should never be more than five years old.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: What about using subitems off the items for each ‘revision’? That way you can see over time the changes? Or set automations to duplicate a group that’s a template of tasks and the previous tasks would be in a ‘done group’?

posts.author: Cellblock-D (Marty Bartram)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks. I will play with both of those options.
